subject: Civics
chapter_name: Parliament and the Making of Laws
overall_learning_objectives: Know the importance of Parliament in a democracy
Identify the two houses of Parliament
Understand the role of Members of Parliament
Learn how laws are made in Parliament
Recognize the role of debates and discussions in decision making

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Parliament
student_activities: Students discuss how leaders represent people
Share examples of issues people want the government to solve

lesson_plan.title: Structure of Parliament
student_activities: Write the names of the two houses of Parliament
Draw a simple diagram showing the structure of Parliament

lesson_plan.title: Members of Parliament
topics_covered: Role of MPs in representing people
student_activities: Discuss how MPs raise issues of their constituencies

lesson_plan.title: Role of the Opposition
student_activities: Students discuss why questioning leaders is important
List two roles of the opposition

lesson_plan.title: Making of Laws
topics_covered: Role of Parliament in passing laws
student_activities: Write the steps of law making
Discuss why debates are important before passing laws

teacher_tip: Use simple examples such as classroom decision making to explain how discussions and voting help in making laws.
